misc.title: ¡¡Nuevo amiibo para la colección!! La semana pasada me quedé con las ganas de comprar el amiibo de “Capitán Toad y la flor parlante” de la colección de “Super Mario Bros. Wonder”, así que hoy me he pa
misc.text: ¡¡Nuevo amiibo para la colección!! La semana pasada me quedé con las ganas de comprar el amiibo de “Capitán Toad y la flor parlante” de la colección de “Super Mario Bros. Wonder”, así que hoy me he pasado a por él a @MediaMarkt_es para quitarme esa espinita 😍😍😍😍😍 Sergio (redon) (@Mugiwara_23) Aunque ayer no compré el “polémico” palomitero de Yoshi (porque 36€ me pareció excesivamente caro) sí que me pasé por @MediaMarkt_es antes de ir al cine y compré el amiibo de “Mario Elefante” de “Super Mario Bros. Wonder” 😍😍😍😍😍 Y creo que no será el último que compre 🤣🤣🤣 — https://nitter.net/Mugiwara_23/status/2039682269518999574#m

misc.title: Thank you for the info from official website!🧡 Netflix says One Piece remake anime will be released “soon,” but the Japanese term,“kinjitsu”, is really vague as you mentioned, It can refer to anythin
misc.text: Thank you for the info from official website!🧡 Netflix says One Piece remake anime will be released “soon,” but the Japanese term,“kinjitsu”, is really vague as you mentioned, It can refer to anything from days or weeks to several months, sometimes even longer. Notably, 2024-2025 reports *never* used this term, which suggests the release is getting closer. My bet is that it will be released within about 6 months.🔥 あれ吉【Are-kichi】 (@SReminal) In Japanese, “kinjitsu” (近日 / “soon”) usually means within a few days or about a week in everyday conversation, but there’s no fixed timeframe. So depending on the person and the situation, it can mean slightly different things. 🙂‍↔️ #ONEPIECE — https://nitter.net/SReminal/status/2041542239558701296#m

Notably, 2024-2025 reports *never* used this term, which suggests the release is getting closer. My bet is that it will be released within about 6 months.🔥 あれ吉【Are-kichi】 (@SReminal) In Japanese, “kinjitsu” (近日 / “soon”) usually means within a few days or about a week in everyday conversation, but there’s no fixed timeframe.
